endpoints.name: Stone Haven Medic (BFZ-51) — wave 41 suspicion REJECTED

description: Maintaining refusal. Worst proposed Gideon-anchor since the last one — wave 41 vision agent stamped Stone Haven Medic with suspected_ip: Gideon Jura at LOW confidence because tall muscular figure healing in a cave. Long pale hair. Gideon Jura's canonical visual identifier across the entire BFZ block is SHORT DARK HAIR. The Stone Havens are canonical kor settlement infrastructure per Cliffside Lookout's flavor. A long-pale-haired healer at a Stone-Havens-named card is a kor field-medic, not the planeswalker. Suspicion cleared in frontmatter. Total Gideon-anchor count holds at 1 depiction (Gideon's Reproach BFZ-30) + 1 flavor-speaker (Smite the Monstrous BFZ-49) = below the standard 2-depictions threshold. Considered the Valgavoth precedent (referenced-not-depicted-but-load-bearing) and rejected it on structural-symmetry grounds: Valgavoth's case was load-bearing because Duskmourn's whole arc thesis routes through him; Gideon's BFZ presence is one beat in a Gatewatch ensemble where four other planeswalkers (Chandra, Nissa, Kiora, Jace) all sit at the same low-anchor count, so singling out Gideon would break the ensemble's structural symmetry. The Ajani-wave-35 precedent holds. Refused.
citations: In-corpus Gideon's Reproach BFZ-30 vision body (canonical short-dark-hair Gideon reference)
In-corpus Cliffside Lookout BFZ-20 flavor text ('Stone Havens' canonical kor settlement infrastructure)
Wave 35 Ajani Goldmane refusal precedent (1 depicted + 2 flavor-speakers = below threshold)
Wave 39 Valgavoth precedent (referenced-not-depicted-but-load-bearing — distinguished on structural-symmetry grounds)

MTG Wiki: Battle for Zendikar story archive (Gatewatch ensemble context)
